steps.title: Spot the appositive
steps.content: The phrase “A conceptual artist and designer embraced by both the art world and the fashion world” precedes “Mary Ping,” followed by the verb “was chosen.” This is an appositive structure—the phrase describes and renames the noun that follows.

steps.title: Apply the comma rule
steps.content:
A comma separates an appositive from its noun, and choice D (world,) correctly inserts this comma between the appositive and “Mary Ping.” Choices A, B, and C fail: A omits the required comma, while B and C use punctuation (colon and semicolon) that has no role in punctuating appositives.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
